food_pairing.name: Alsace Riesling with choucroute garnie
food_pairing.pairing: Dry Alsace Riesling with Strasbourg choucroute garnie
food_pairing.description: The textbook Alsace pairing. Riesling's racy acidity cuts through the pork brine, lard and slow-cooked kraut while its citrus and stone lift the dish's saltiness without competing.

food_pairing.name: Alsace Gewurztraminer with Munster cheese
food_pairing.pairing: Off-dry Alsace Gewurztraminer with Munster cheese (with caraway or cumin)
food_pairing.description: The classic Vosges valley pairing. Munster's pungent washed-rind cream meets Gewurz's rose, lychee and ginger; the cheese's traditional caraway or cumin echoes the wine's exotic spice.

food_pairing.name: Alsace Riesling with coq au Riesling
food_pairing.pairing: Alsace Riesling with the wine-braised chicken of the same name
food_pairing.description: Coq au Riesling is the Alsace cousin of coq au vin, braised in dry Riesling and finished with cream. The same wine in the glass amplifies the dish's herbal lift and creamy backbone.

food_pairing.name: Alsace Pinot Blanc with tarte flambee
food_pairing.pairing: Light Alsace Pinot Blanc or Edelzwicker with tarte flambee
food_pairing.description: Tarte flambee, the Alsace cream-and-bacon flatbread, calls for a gentle, refreshing white. Pinot Blanc-Auxerrois soft fruit and salt frame the creme-fraiche and lardons without overshadowing.

food_pairing.name: Alsace Pinot Gris with baeckeoffe
food_pairing.pairing: Off-dry Alsace Pinot Gris with baeckeoffe (three-meat baker's casserole)
food_pairing.description: Baeckeoffe is the Alsace casserole of lamb, beef and pork marinated in Riesling and slow-baked. Pinot Gris's smoky pear, weight and gentle off-dryness carry the long-cooked meats and root vegetables.

food_pairing.name: Gewurztraminer or Pinot Gris with foie gras
food_pairing.pairing: Late-harvest Gewurztraminer or SGN Pinot Gris with foie gras
food_pairing.description: The Alsace luxury pairing. Honeyed exotic Vendanges Tardives Gewurztraminer or SGN Pinot Gris cradles foie gras's richness while the wine's acidity stops the combination from cloying.

food_pairing.name: Cremant d Alsace with bretzel aperitif
food_pairing.pairing: Brut Cremant d Alsace with hot bretzel and aperitif charcuterie
food_pairing.description: The bar-stool Alsace ritual. Cremant's traditional-method bubbles and biscuit lees ageing meet the warm pretzel's salt; a perfect winstub aperitif before the choucroute lands.

food_pairing.name: Alsace Pinot Noir with presskopf
food_pairing.pairing: Alsace Pinot Noir or light red with presskopf (head cheese)
food_pairing.description: Presskopf, the Alsace pork head terrine cured in vinegar and herbs, finds a peer in Pinot Noir's cherry, acid and gentle tannin; the wine cuts the rich aspic without overpowering.

food_pairing.name: Alsace Gewurztraminer with creamy spaetzle
food_pairing.pairing: Off-dry Gewurztraminer with cheese spaetzle and caramelised onion
food_pairing.description: Egg spaetzle with melted Emmental or Munster and caramelised onion is a winstub staple. Gewurztraminer's gentle sweetness lifts the cheese and onion while its acidity stops the dish from feeling heavy.

food_pairing.name: Dry Muscat with white asparagus
food_pairing.pairing: Dry Alsace Muscat with white asparagus and hollandaise
food_pairing.description: Dry Muscat is one of the few wines that handles white asparagus, the seasonal Alsace vegetable. Its grape-on-the-vine lift mirrors the spear's vegetal sweetness rather than fighting it.

food_pairing.name: Alsace Sylvaner with quiche Lorraine
food_pairing.pairing: Dry Alsace Sylvaner with quiche Lorraine and a green salad
food_pairing.description: Sylvaner's herb, salt and bright acid frame the cream-and-bacon classic. The wine's understated personality is the antidote to Riesling overload at a Sunday lunch.

food_pairing.name: Vendanges Tardives Gewurztraminer with kougelhopf
food_pairing.pairing: Vendanges Tardives Gewurztraminer with kougelhopf (yeasted brioche cake)
food_pairing.description: Kougelhopf, the rum-soaked yeasted brioche cake of Alsace, finds its match in honeyed late-harvest Gewurz. The wine's exotic fruit and rose petal weave through the cake's almond and raisin.

food_pairing.name: Grand Cru Riesling with plateau de fruits de mer
food_pairing.pairing: Grand Cru Alsace Riesling with a plateau of raw oysters, crab and shellfish
food_pairing.description: Top-tier Alsace Riesling, with its saline mineral and racy acid, is the great non-Champagne French shellfish wine. Granite and volcanic Grand Crus particularly amplify oyster brine.

food_pairing.name: Alsace Pinot Gris with roast pork and apples
food_pairing.pairing: Off-dry Pinot Gris with roast pork loin and apples
food_pairing.description: Pinot Gris's smoky pear, almond and gentle off-dryness mirror the apple sweetness of a herbed roast pork loin. A classic Alsace Sunday-lunch pairing.

food_pairing.name: Klevener de Heiligenstein with white tarte flambee
food_pairing.pairing: Klevener de Heiligenstein with tarte flambee specials (mushroom, gruyere)
food_pairing.description: Klevener de Heiligenstein, the rare Savagnin Rose appellation north of Andlau, gives an aromatic but restrained white that handles the white tarte flambee variants with extra cheese or mushroom.

food_pairing.name: Natural Pinot Noir with pain d epices glazed pork
food_pairing.pairing: Natural Alsace Pinot Noir with pork glazed in pain d epices spice
food_pairing.description: Pain d epices, the Alsace spice bread of honey, anise and cinnamon, often glazes a pork rib. A light natural Pinot Noir matches the warming spice without competing for weight.

food_pairing.name: Complanted Grand Cru with roast goose
food_pairing.pairing: Complanted Alsace Grand Cru with roast goose and chestnut stuffing
food_pairing.description: The complanted Grand Crus of Marcel Deiss, Binner and the Ribeauville cooperative pair the noble grapes for a multi-axis aromatic profile that handles the richness of festive game bird.

food_pairing.name: Alsace orange wine with spiced washed-rind cheese or curry
food_pairing.pairing: Skin-macerated Alsace orange wine with Munster or mild Indian curry
food_pairing.description: Alsace orange wine (skin-macerated Pinot Gris or field blend) has gentle tannic grip and dried apricot weight that loves pungent washed-rind cheese and mild curries with cumin and coriander.
